hero.title: The control panel for your development projects.
hero.subtitle: Teams, projects, tasks, documentation and deploy in one place.

features.title: Built for developers who want order

f1.title: Organized projects
f1.desc: Tasks, docs, files and activity per project — not across 5 tools.

f2.title: Teams with roles
f2.desc: Owner, admin, developer or viewer. Each one sees what they should.

f3.title: Guided documentation
f3.desc: Section checklist and an automatically generated README.

f4.title: Deploy Wizard
f4.desc: Clear steps to ship to Vercel without tripping.

f5.title: AI assistant
f5.desc: Ask Clippy anything. Instant FAQ + DeepSeek when you need more.

f6.title: Stack detection
f6.desc: DevHub reads your repo and figures out the stack. The base of the Deploy Wizard.

f7.title: Built-in GitHub
f7.desc: Commits, branches and issues without leaving DevHub. Create issues from the app.

f8.title: Roles that hold
f8.desc: OWNER · ADMIN · DEVELOPER · VIEWER. Enforced server-side, not only in the UI.

register.title: Create your account
register.subtitle: Get started for free with DevHub
register.name: Name

empty.title: No activity yet
empty.desc: Create your first project to start using DevHub.

readme.title: What is a README?
readme.body: A README is the main documentation file of a project. It explains what the system does, how it is installed, how it runs and which technologies it uses.

project.title: What is a project?
project.body: A project groups together tasks, documentation, members, files and integrations related to an application or product under development.

team.title: What is a team?
team.body: A team represents a group of users that collaborate on one or more projects. Each member can have a different role.

saas.title: What is SaaS?
saas.body: SaaS stands for Software as a Service. It's a model where users access an application over the internet and usually pay through plans, limits or features.

deploy.title: What is deploy?
deploy.body: Deploying is the process of publishing an application in an environment that's accessible to users, usually on the internet.

envVar.title: What is an environment variable?
envVar.body: An environment variable stores configuration that changes per environment, such as URLs, private keys or connection data. It must not be exposed publicly.
